title: Engineering harness retrospective
description: Schema for a single session-end retrospective. Designed to be filled in at the end of meaningful agent or human work and committed to harness/state/friction-log.md (or kept locally if the team prefers). The schema enforces concrete answers — minLength guards keep the form honest.

workedWell.description: What materially helped this session — a command, a fixture, a diagnostic, a default that meant you didn't have to think. Concrete only; avoid generalities.

confusing.description: What got in the way or sent the session sideways — an unclear error, a missing command, a stale doc, a flaky check. Concrete only; this becomes a friction-log entry candidate.

magicWand.description: If you had a magic wand, what ONE thing would you change to make the next run easier, safer, faster, higher quality, or better proven? Be concrete — name a command, flag, output field, fixture, diagnostic, template, sensor, check, or workflow change.

magicWandTarget.description: Where the magic-wand change would live. project = the product code or its environment; harness = this repo's engineering harness (the most actionable category); agent = the model runtime or session control (usually out of this repo's reach).

inferenceGap.description: What did the agent or reviewer have to infer that the harness should have proved? Name the missing signal, sensor, check, smoke flow, or evidence path.

proofLevel.description: Optional. Highest proof level reached this session on the proof-level ladder (L0 nothing observed → L6 reproducible behavioural proof). Distinct from the harness-maturity ladder (L0–L4) in HARNESS.md.

harnessCommandsRun.description: Optional. Subcommands invoked this session (e.g. ['doctor', 'validate --tier quick', 'health']). Useful for review.
